statement: A continuously differentiable function on `[a,b]` is Lipschitz on `[a,b]`.
lean_statement:
theorem exists_lipschitzOnWith_of_contDiffOn
    (f : Real → Real) (a b : Real) (hf : ContDiffOn Real 1 f (Set.Icc a b)) :
    ∃ L : NNReal, LipschitzOnWith L f (Set.Icc a b)
proof:
Proof: since `f` is `C^1` on the compact interval `[a,b]`, its derivative `f'` is continuous there,
hence bounded: let `M = sup_{x ∈ [a,b]} |f' x|`. For any `x,y ∈ [a,b]`, the mean value theorem gives
`|f x - f y| ≤ M * |x - y|`. Thus `f` is Lipschitz on `[a,b]` with Lipschitz constant `M`.
